title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After the man dies, Bobby Jones uses a silk handkerchief to cover the corpse's face. While doing so, he discovers a photograph of a beautiful young woman in the man's pocket, an image that stays intensely in his mind. As Bobby waits for the doctor to return, he feels anxious about being late for his commitment to play the organ for the six o'clock evening prayer. He reflects on his relationship with his father, Thomas Jones, a neurotic vicar whose digestive health suffers when he is flustered; Bobby views their relationship as a cycle of misunderstood sacrifices.

A stranger named Basil Frenchie, a man of about thirty-five wearing golf pants, arrives on the narrow platform where the body lies. Bobby explains the situation and asks Frenchie to watch over the corpse so that he can attend the church service. Bobby then climbs the steep path and sprints across the fields, jumping over a courtyard wall in a manner that displeases the parish priest.

At the church, Bobby plays Chopin's Funeral March on the organ. Following the service, Thomas Jones reprimands Bobby for his lack of punctuality and responsibility. Bobby attempts to lighten the mood by joking that he was "busy with a corpse" near the 17th hole. He explains that the man fell off a cliff and died after the doctor left. Thomas is horrified by Bobby's perceived apathy and lack of solemnity toward death. While Bobby attributes his father's reactions to the psychological effects of the war, Thomas feels ashamed of his son's perceived indifference. The two men walk to the priest's residence, both internally making excuses for the other.
